seoTitle: How to Cancel Trello Premium Subscription
metaDescription: Cancel Trello Premium from the Workspace billing settings, skip retention offers, and confirm your downgrade to the free tier is complete.
intro: Trello bills at the Workspace level rather than the individual profile, which means cancelling requires admin access and knowing where the billing settings live. This guide walks through downgrading your Workspace from Premium back to the free plan.

steps.heading: Confirm you have admin access
steps.body: Billing settings are locked to Workspace Administrators, so make sure your account has that level of access before proceeding.

steps.heading: Open your Workspace billing settings
steps.body: Select your target Premium workspace in the sidebar, then click the Billing tab.

steps.heading: Review your plan details
steps.body: Check your next scheduled billing date and total seat costs before making changes.

steps.heading: Find the cancel option
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom of the billing page to locate the Cancel or Downgrade link.

steps.heading: Decline retention offers
steps.body: Click through the feedback survey screens and decline any promotional discounts if you want to proceed with cancellation.

steps.heading: Confirm the cancellation
steps.body: Click Cancel Subscription to finalize the downgrade, then check your account settings for a confirmation that your plan will end on a specific date.

faq.question: Will I lose my boards if I cancel Trello Premium?
faq.answer: No, your board data stays intact and simply reverts to the limitations of the free tier.

faq.question: Who can cancel a Trello Premium subscription?
faq.answer: Only Workspace Administrators have access to the billing menu needed to cancel or downgrade a plan.

faq.question: What features will I lose after downgrading?
faq.answer: Advanced views like Timeline or Dashboard typically deactivate once you move back to the free tier.

faq.question: How do I know my cancellation went through?
faq.answer: Check your account settings for a confirmation message showing your plan will cancel on a specific date, and watch for an email invoicing document.
